title: Embodied Learning for Literacy EEG
description: This EEG dataset comprises neurophysiological recordings from children during a literacy intervention study examining embodied learning effects on letter discrimination. Participants completed a two-alternative forced choice discrimination task before and after an intervention period, with concurrent visual search task performance during the intervention phase. The dataset includes 21 participants divided into intervention and control groups, with event markers indicating stimulus type (letters b and d) and trial classification.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected during three experimental stages: pre-intervention letter discrimination task, intervention-phase visual search task with target and distractor letters, and post-intervention letter discrimination task. Stimulus presentation events were marked with codes indicating letter type (100 for b, 200 for d) and trial type (10 for b practice, 20 for d practice).
